content_type: cinematic utopian montage (Morning in America homage)
summary: Department of Labor video set to music, opening with the narration 'It's morning again in America' over color graded footage of work. It cuts between a worker in a hard hat walking through an industrial plant, the engraved 'United States Department of Labor' building, a steelworker in ear protection looking off-frame, an American flag hoisted by a construction crane atop an industrial tower, and a closing shot of the DOL seal over a waving flag. The narration includes 'the American worker is what makes America great... prouder, stronger, and working again.'

script.scene: Worker in a white hard hat and dark jacket walks away from the camera through a large industrial plant with overhead lighting and gantry structures. Narration: 'It's morning again in America. Across the country, Americans are getting up and getting to work.'

Low-angle shot of the engraved bronze sign 'United States Department of Labor, 200 Constitution Avenue NW,' the Frances Perkins Building behind it, with light flares. Narration about supporting the American worker and strengthening the workforce.

Close-up of a Black steelworker in an orange hard hat and ear protection, looking off-frame with warm backlighting. Narration: 'From job training to safer workplaces, we're helping Americans build a future they can count on.'

A large American flag is hoisted by a construction crane to the top of an industrial tower in mist. Narration: 'the American worker keeps America moving, and the American worker is what makes America great.'

Closing graphic: the U.S. Department of Labor seal over a slowly waving American flag, sepia tone. Narration: 'under the leadership of President Trump, our country is prouder, stronger, and working again... we put America first by putting American workers first.'

notable_text: 'It's morning again in America'; 'the American worker is what makes America great'; 'prouder, stronger, and working again'; 'we put America first by putting American workers first'; tweet: 'Thanks to @POTUS, America is back'
